item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome idea and awesome work congratulations!!!! +1

item.title: SEVENTY CHILDREN
item.content:encoded:
I loved the story until the very end. It’s such a little button to end on. “Oh, people are going missing, MTF found the creatures, and oh yeah 70 children died.”

Lovely.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: The journey of a thousand upvotes begins with a single character.

item.title: delightfully creepy
item.content:encoded:
It is very clever set up for a con man. This old sage like creature is literally talking out of it's ass telling kids that their the chosen hero. This is funny in a Dark sort of way and an excellent cautionary tale.
The critter reminds me of my favorite anime monster, Elder Centipede from One Punch Man and the face stealer from Avatar the Last Airbender. The story also reminds me of a recent episode of The Owl House. It was called Witches before Wizards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone's talking about the horror of digestion or the whole butt centipede thing, but the thing that gets me is that fantasy is used as the lure for these creatures; the thing we use to escape the horrors of reality being used by said horrors to their own unconscious ends.

Yeah I don't like that one bit, easy upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hate to run into one of these +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes on all counts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I’m having trouble visualizing this.
1. Centipede shoves head and ass in ground, and raises up its body in arch shape.
2. Ass pops back up nearby, deploys sock puppet flaps (?). Ass looks like a face (?? somehow???)
3. Sock puppet ass lures people through the archway to be eaten.
Is… that correct?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When these flaps are fully extended, SCP-4310's exposed posterior segment roughly resembles the face of an old man (with movement of its rear-most legs simulating motion of the 'mouth' and 'jaw').

Its tail segment produces a repeating series of recognizable English phrases that resemble a heroic speech (typically describing a quest or prophecy that must be fulfilled by entering 'the archway').

These things are literally talking out of their asses.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nitpicks:

keratin flaps

Keratin is only found in animals with an internal skeleton. For arthropods like centipedes it'd be chitin.

warm-blooded mammals

All mammals are warm blooded. If you mean to include birds, that should be "warm-blooded animals". If you don't want to include birds, just "mammals" is enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's fair. This is deliberately meant to be a black comedy — the humor comes from subverting the trope of children entering a fantasy world — but comedy is wholly subjective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a lot of what-the-fuck in this article. Giant centipedes, cool (I hate centipedes). Feeding by creating inter-dimensional doorways? Sure, fine. Mimicking the speech of a wizard, while looking like a floating old-man-head next to a arch?? It's too much.

I want to like this article more, because I'm already terrified of normal centipedes. But these ones just seem goofy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very cool, thank you for the translation!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good example of how you can put a lot of substance into a pretty short article and make it stand out. Very creepy and disgusting things, especially for someone with entomophobia.

Upvoted. And translated into russian too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the Mimic from Dark Souls but it has some weird, grotesque, fantastical nature to it that makes it stand out and appear unique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Short, gross, awful.

+1

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: And in less than two days! A new personal best, and I’m super grateful to Hippo for polishing this absolute diamond in the rough!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point, I completely blanked on the DNA/1950s thing. Blood-type is a little odd for matching in animal leavings, though — I'll change it to teeth, instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did the foundation have DNA tests in 1950? Small detail, but it may make more sense to have matched blood type back then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Come hither worthy explorer!"

Oh no, wait it's just a giant centipede.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a side-note: A Random Day may have his own reasons, but my thinking behind excluding a transcript of their 'speech' is to discourage anthropomorphism of the centipedes. I don't want readers to think they're consciously "fooling" the children; I want readers to realize these are just the sounds the centipede makes when it wants food to walk into its mouth.

And while you could accomplish the latter with a transcript that wasn't quite "right" (an imperfect natural 'mimicry' of heroic speeches), it both ran counter to what ARD wanted (he didn't want botnik 2.0, as I understood it) and, I think, creates another layer of mystery (how the hell do these 'speeches' occur, especially since the centipedes don't even know what they are — they use them for mating calls, for chrissakes!).

I agree that it's a slight (but notable!) break in realism to exclude the speeches; I see it as a concession to prioritizing atmosphere over verisimilitude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ireland was an afterthought, honestly — specifically because I wanted the first sighting to be the birth-place of C. S. Lewis (for the extra irony). I imagine it's more commonly found in Great Britain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small thought: as the creature is noted as native to "Great Britain and Ireland" (recognising therefore that Ireland =/= part of GB, regardless of the makeup of "Team GB"), and the first instance (quite possibly the one in containment) was discovered in (/recovered from) Ireland, would the containment environment be better described as resembling "the temperate forests of the British Isles", rather than "of Great Britain"? The former including Ireland. Though I don't know which option would be preferred by the actual Irish…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… uh… I wanna puke…

Thank you Hippo, for continuing the trend of scaring me shitless with your skips.

+1 for both of you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It really should, however, User-Curated Lists doesn't accept any articles in an active contest.

Afterwards, definitely — there should be enough good works in this vein in recent months to add another 25 to FFT.
And in less than thirteen hours! Short articles with cliches still have legs - many, many, many legs!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love how minimalist this is while creating a memorable anomaly and powerful image. I respectfully disagree that more content would make it better; the imagination is put to work enthusiased by the building blocks here.

Personally, I think the child deathing is a bit too easy of a move, esp as one to end on, but the article at least justifies it well as an inevitability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is horribly amusing. It made me think of the feeling I had about the SCP where wild animals attack you in the toilet - 'the universe created this just because it hates you'. Easy +1.

I agree with Cyantreuse that this could be improved with a little more content (some nasty pictures particularly). The lack of specific transcripts of the speeches is also a noticeable omission. I'm not sure whether adding some would improve this as a piece of fictional writing, but I would expect them to be included in a 'non-fiction' report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Geez, there have been so many awesome team ups for this contest, makes me regret not joining in.

+1, guys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is some seriously minimalist Series I deconstructionist flash horror you've got there. This really should be added to the Flash Fiction Throwbacks list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this concept, but I feel like it deserves more content. There are elements to it that are fun to visualize — like the centipedes' "enter the archway" announcements — but they only get the brief summary description, and I don't feel like that uses the uniqueness here to its full potential. Novote for now. Edit: Seeing it described as Series I throwback is fair, so maybe what I'm looking for just isn't this article's intended style.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Combined with the keratin flaps, it makes the head of the centipede appear to be an old man in robes — a wizard. Throw in the call, and this "wizard" is giving children a "quest" to enter the "archway".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only question I have is about the whole "Old man" shape it can take. It never gets utilized in the SCP proper, as far as I can tell, so what's the point of it?

I'm probably missing something wildly obvious here, but it's 5AM so my poor old brain needs some help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, but delightfully fucked up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Simple and spooky, well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks I hate it.

Take your upvote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, those Modern Brothers Grimm fairytales. +1

item.title: The Hero with a Thousand Legs
item.content:encoded:
Cliches covered:

  • Compulsion effect that makes you do bad things/kills you.
  • Spooky scary murder monster(s)!
  • A doorway of any shape to a different dimension/timeline/world.

I didn't intend to write a Cliche-Con entry. Then this image popped almost fully formed into my head barely twelve hours ago. I wrote the first draft up within less than an hour; about nine hours later, Hippo was kind enough to revise it so extensively that he became a de facto coauthor.

Many thanks to Captain Kirby, Roget , Tufto, and WrongJohnSilver for their feedback, and here's to another year of writing SCPs!

item.title: authors' posts
item.content:encoded:
What has become of Millicent Frastley?
Is there any hope that she is still alive?
Why haven’t they found her?
It's rather ghastly to think
The little child was not yet five…!

SOURCES:

  • Image here.

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
